task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into the NOSH ChartingSystem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NOSH ChartingSystem dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating the agent is no longer on the login screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the patient record for PID 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient chart or demographic page is open. The VLM should look for PID 3 or the name 'Crysta Parisian' (the actual database name for PID 3) in the patient header.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the clinical reminders section for the patient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A section, tab, or modal specifically designated for 'Reminders' or 'Clinical Reminders' is open within the patient's chart.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered 'Mammogram' as the reminder content and 'October 1, 2025' as the due date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A reminder creation form is filled out with 'Mammogram' in the reminder/reason field and '10/01/2025', '2025-10-01', or 'October 1, 2025' in the date field.

task_completion.description: Verify the reminder was successfully saved or scheduled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is displayed, or the new reminder for 'Mammogram' due on 'October 1, 2025' appears in the patient's list of active/scheduled reminders.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web GUI to schedule the reminder.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the NOSH UI, rather than a terminal window executing backend scripts or curl commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual NOSH ChartingSystem application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The surrounding UI elements (menus, patient headers, application styling) match a genuine web application and not a text editor or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: Verify the reminder was added to the correct patient (PID 3).
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient header or ID displayed on the screen during the reminder creation corresponds to PID 3 (or Crysta Parisian), ensuring the agent didn't just create a new patient or use a random one.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target patient PID is 3. Note that in the system database, PID 3 corresponds to the name 'Crysta Parisian', not 'Lucinda Haag' as stated in the task description. The VLM should accept actions performed on PID 3 / Crysta Parisian as correct.
